chapter_title.ar: باب أَكْلِ مَا يَسْقُطُ مِنَ الْخِوَانِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 49 – Eat what falls from the food-spread

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/117)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/117)
text: أَكَلْنَا عِنْدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَلَمَّا رُفِعَ الْخِوَانُ لَقَطَ مَا وَقَعَ مِنْهُ فَأَكَلَهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَنَا إِنَّهُ يَنْفِي الْفَقْرَ وَ يُكْثِرُ الْوَلَدَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Mansour Bin Al Abbas, from Al Hassan Bin Muawiya Bin Wahab, from his father, who said,

‘We ate in the presence of Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>. So when the meal was raised, he<sup>asws</sup> picked up what had fallen from it, so he<sup>asws</sup> ate it. Then he<sup>asws</sup> said to us: ‘It negates the poverty and causes abundance of the children’.
en.sarwar: 4. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Mansur ibn al-‘Abbas from al-Hassan ibn Wahab from his father who has said the following: “Once we ate food with abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam. When the table-spread was collected, he (the Imam) picked up the pieces that had fallen on it and ate them then said to us, ‘This removes poverty and increases the number of children.’”
